design_philosophy.core_loop: Vertically scroll through stages, destroy air and ground targets with missiles and bombs, collect power-ups from bunkers, manage limited bomb supply, reach helipad for bonus points
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing resource scarcity (bombs) against enemy density; managing helicopter durability across stages with limited lives
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control an attack helicopter engaging military targets across varied terrains with tactical weapon selection
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate design choice to use slow helicopter craft for strategic route planning and bullet visibility; bomb mechanic as risk/reward resource that can be detonated by enemy fire; explicit design philosophy to balance stress with excitement and maintain replayability after death

difficulty.lives_system: Three starting lives; additional lives awarded via score thresholds or destroying ten diamond-shaped blocks
difficulty.continues_system: Hidden continue option in NES version allows resume from last stage reached
difficulty.checkpointing: Player sent back to checkpoint upon death within stage
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Score-based extra lives create skill-based difficulty scaling; bomb management and gunner recruitment provide resource trade-offs; secret targets offer bonus scoring for exploration

progression.upgrade_system: Dynamic formation system: recruit up to two small helicopter gunners (red for lateral fire, gray for forward fire) by shooting red/gray bunkers; bomb replenishment via green bunker pickups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stages unlock sequentially; after final stage, game loops to stage two with increased difficulty
replayability_hooks: Scoring system with hidden secret targets for bonus points
Different gunner combinations create varied firepower configurations
Score-based extra lives encourage high-score runs
Difficulty loop after stage four increases challenge for continued play
Hidden continue system enables stage-specific practice

world_building_techniques: Environmental stage variety (multiple terrains across four stages)
Enemy type diversity suggests military conflict scenario (tanks, gunboats, artillery, aircraft)
Thematic soundtrack composed to convey sense of bravery

faction_indicators: Enemy variety suggests organized military opposition (tanks, artillery, gunboats)
danger_signals: Limited bomb supply creates resource scarcity tension
environmental_storytelling: Stage progression through varied terrains (implied geographic campaign)
Target variety (military installations, vehicles, infrastructure) suggests wartime scenario
Helipad landing points serve as stage boundaries and resupply checkpoints

sources_used: wikipedia: Tiger-Heli article (Gameplay section, Development section)
